method: ratio = 1 + national_dependent_load × age_factor × dual_coverage_haircut, then shrunk toward the naive 1.9× anchor by λ. The age factor re-weights a relative dependent-load shape (by worker age band) to the workforce's own age marginal, anchored so the national age mix returns 1.0. The dual-coverage haircut removes, for working-spouse / own-ESI households, the spouse and ~half their children from this employer's dependent load.

sources.note: National covered-lives-per-worker anchor (~1.9); working-spouse rules/surcharges among firms ≥200 workers (~10% exclude, ~13% surcharge).
Dual-offer availability among married workers (spousal own-ESI access).
Workforce age marginal driving the age factor.

limitations: DEP_SHAPE is an illustrative relative shape, not a measured age×dependent table; replace with ACS PUMS household composition for the member geography to remove the assumption.
The spouse-decline rate is a conservative national default; an employer's actual working-spouse rule (if any) would set it precisely.
Public-data estimate only — reconciled against the client's census (Mode 1B), whose ground-truth covered lives is held as reconciliation_covered_lives.
